title: Credit & Debit Cards
heroImageAlt: A traveller holding a credit card beside a laptop while paying online
intro: We accept all major Sri Lankan and international credit and debit cards through a secure, PCI-DSS-compliant payment gateway.

sections.p:
VISA, MASTERCARD and AMERICAN EXPRESS issued by Sri Lankan banks including Sampath Bank, Commercial Bank of Ceylon, Hatton National Bank (HNB), Nations Trust Bank, Bank of Ceylon, People’s Bank, NDB, DFCC, Seylan Bank, Standard Chartered, HSBC, Cargills Bank, Pan Asia Bank and Union Bank.

International cards from any country are also accepted, subject to your card’s authorisation rules for online purchases in Sri Lanka.

A small convenience fee applies on card payments to cover the gateway and merchant costs: 2.0% on Visa/Mastercard, 2.6% on American Express. The fee is shown on the payment page before you confirm.

To avoid the card fee, pay by bank transfer (fee-free) — see the Bank Transfer page.

Card payments are processed in real time. Once your bank approves the transaction, your booking is ticketed immediately and the e-ticket is emailed to you within minutes.

If the gateway times out or the bank declines the transaction, the booking is held for a few minutes so you can try again with the same card or a different one — no charge is made until a successful authorisation.

Every card payment is authenticated with 3-D Secure: after entering your card details you will be redirected to your bank’s page to enter a one-time password sent by SMS or generated by the bank’s app.

This means even if your card details were somehow compromised elsewhere, an attacker would still need access to your phone or banking app to charge them.

sections.h: What we store — and what we do not
sections.p:
We DO NOT store your full card number, your CVV or your expiry date on our own servers. The payment gateway holds the card data; we hold only a non-reversible TOKEN representing the card, which lets us refer to the transaction for refunds and support.

This design means that even if our servers were somehow breached, your card details could not be recovered from them.

If your card is declined, the most common reasons are: 1) the card is not enabled for online or international transactions (call your bank to enable), 2) the available credit/balance is below the purchase amount plus fee, 3) the bank’s fraud system has flagged the unusual purchase (call your bank to authorise).

In each case the problem is on the issuing bank’s side — we do not see the reason, only the decline code. Calling your bank usually resolves it within minutes and you can re-submit the payment.
